children.text: The most popular mobile payment method in Poland. BLIK is a system embedded in banking applications and covered by all major Polish banks. Currently, over 90% of all customers of Polish financial institutions have the option to use BLIK in mobile banking applications.

excerpt: Poland’s popular bank-embedded mobile payment method using one-time codes and in-app confirmation.

content_features.title: Strong local coverage in Poland
content_features.description: BLIK is widely available through major Polish banks’ mobile apps, making it a familiar checkout option for local shoppers.

content_features.title: Mobile-first authorization flow
content_features.description: Customers generate a one-time BLIK code in their banking app and confirm the payment, reducing the need to enter long payment details.

content_features.title: Approval rates and declines
content_features.description: Authorization success can depend on correct code entry, code expiration timing, and customer confirmation in their banking app—optimize UX to reduce avoidable declines.

content_features.title: Security via in-app confirmation
content_features.description: Transactions are confirmed in the shopper’s banking app, adding an authentication step without relying on card details.

content_features.title: Refund support (provider/PSP dependent)
content_features.description: Refund availability and timing depend on your PSP and integration; verify supported operations (full/partial) in your provider documentation.

content_features.title: Disputes and chargeback handling
content_features.description: Dispute processes vary by PSP and scheme rules; ensure you capture order and customer evidence to respond effectively.

content_features.title: Online acceptance
content_features.description: BLIK supports ecommerce and other online payment flows.

content_features.title: In-person acceptance
content_features.description: BLIK supports in-person checkout and POS acceptance.

tip: Reduce BLIK declines by clearly prompting customers to enter the 6-digit code promptly and then confirm the payment in their banking app.

faq.question: What is BLIK?
faq.answer: BLIK is a Polish account-to-account and code-based payment method used for ecommerce and other bank-linked checkout flows.

faq.question: Where is BLIK available?
faq.answer: BLIK is primarily available in Poland and is typically used by customers of Polish banks that support BLIK in their mobile apps.

faq.question: Why are BLIK payments sometimes declined?
faq.answer: Common causes include an incorrect or expired code, the customer not confirming the payment in their banking app in time, or bank-side restrictions. Improve on-screen instructions and timeouts to reduce user-driven declines.

faq.question: Is BLIK a card payment method?
faq.answer: No. BLIK is a bank-embedded mobile payment method and does not require customers to enter card details at checkout.

faq.question: Does BLIK support refunds and partial refunds?
faq.answer: Refund support depends on the PSP and how the payment method is enabled in your integration. Confirm the supported refund operations in your provider’s documentation.

faq.question: What is BLIK?
faq.answer: BLIK is a Polish account-to-account and code-based payment method used for ecommerce and other bank-linked checkout flows.

faq.question: Does BLIK support multiple currencies?
faq.answer: BLIK is primarily used for Polish shopper flows and PLN transactions, while any settlement flexibility depends on your PSP or merchant setup.

seo.seo_description: BLIK overview for merchants covering payments, acceptance, security, and operations.
seo.keywords: BLIK, BLIK payment provider, BLIK approval rate, BLIK card declines, payment network, merchant payments
